channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bwala's comparison of £2,600 UK income with ₦60,000 salary in Nigeria sparks widespread criticism online
item.description: Daniel Bwala, Special Adviser to President Bola Tinubu on Policy Communication, has generated serious public debate after saying that, in some situations, a Nigerian earning ₦60,000 every month may be...

item.title: France and Morocco clash in FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final
item.description: France and Morocco commenced their FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final clash in Boston, with both teams showcasing tactical discipline and resilience. The match, which began at 6:03am, saw early chances...

item.title: ABS-CBN Studios Launches First Vertical Drama 'The Chambermaid’s Daughter' on iWant
item.description: ABS-CBN Studios has officially launched its first vertical drama, 'The Chambermaid’s Daughter,' on the iWant platform, marking a significant step in the evolution of digital content delivery. This dra...

item.title: Economist Suggests Reforming Equalization Payments to Reduce Provincial Tensions
item.description: Trevor Tombe, an economist at the University of Calgary, argues that Canada's current equalization payment system, which bases transfers on provincial revenue generation, fuels resentment between weal...

item.title: Nigeria halts third-party visa services for US applicants
item.description: The Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS) has suspended Online Integrated Solutions (OIS) services, which previously handled visa applications for US citizens. Applicants now must submit their application...

item.title: Angus Taylor criticizes One Nation's fiscal risks and warns of economic turmoil
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor has launched a scathing critique of the One Nation party, warning that if it were to form government, it would plunge Australia into an 'eternity of pain' due to its rec...

item.title: England dominate India with 9-wicket victory to clinch T20I series
item.description: In a thrilling encounter at Bristol, England secured a decisive nine-wicket victory over India in the second T20I of the series. Harry Brook's composed innings of 79 and Phil Salt's explosive 59 off 4...

item.title: Naira depreciates amid seasonal dollar demand for travel and school fees
item.description: The Nigerian naira is experiencing renewed depreciation in the parallel foreign exchange market due to increased demand for dollars driven by summer travel, overseas school fees, and business trips. T...

item.title: The Last Dinner Party release new single ‘Knocking At The Sky’ from ‘From The Pyre’ deluxe edition
item.description: The Last Dinner Party have released their new single ‘Knocking At The Sky’, the final track from their 2025 album ‘From The Pyre’ (deluxe edition). The song, which was debuted at London’s O2 Academy B...

item.title: Political fallout as scandals surrounding candidate Platner intensify
item.description: The article details the escalating political fallout following the revelation of multiple scandals involving Graham Platner, a Democratic Senate candidate in Maine. Platner faced allegations of past m...

item.title: Zambia Vice President Survives Helicopter Crash During Campaign Tour
item.description: Zambia's vice president, Mutale Nalumango, survived a military helicopter crash that occurred minutes after take-off during a campaign tour in Nakonde. The incident happened as she was traveling with ...

item.title: European Parliament Reinstates Tech Companies' Right to Scan Private Messages for Child Abuse Detection
item.description: The European Parliament has voted to extend legislation allowing tech companies like Meta, Google, and Microsoft to scan users’ private messages for child sexual abuse material under the 'Chat Control...

item.title: stutter-step penalty technique in football: a double-edged sword for top players
item.description: the article explores the stutter-step penalty technique in football, a method where players use a rhythmic, exaggerated run-up to deceive goalkeepers. originated in the 1980s by players like pele and ...

item.title: Building Resilient Businesses: Strategies for Economic Uncertainty
item.description: The article discusses insights from Capitec's BizTalk event, which focuses on helping businesses navigate economic challenges. Key themes include managing cash flow, mitigating risk, and maintaining f...

item.title: Tanzania Explores Cashew Shell Value Addition for Economic Growth
item.description: Tanzania's cashew industry is focusing on innovative methods to add value to cashew shells, transforming agricultural waste into profitable products. Cashew shells, previously discarded as byproducts,...

item.title: Nigeria’s Former Minister Seeks to Recover Assets After UK Acquittal
item.description: Former Nigerian Petroleum Minister Diezani Alison-Madueke, acquitted of bribery charges in the UK after an 11-year legal battle, is now focusing on recovering assets seized by Nigeria’s anti-corruptio...

item.title: iWant's 'Miss Behave' faces photo leak controversy as Alexa and Xyriel's drama unfolds
item.description: The upcoming original series 'Miss Behave' on iWant, starring Alexa Ilacad and Xyriel Manabat, is set to premiere on June 3, 2026. However, the project has already encountered challenges, with reports...

item.title: ODM Party Removes Edwin Sifuna as Secretary General
item.description: The Office of the Registrar of Political Parties (ORPP) has officially approved the removal of Edwin Sifuna as Secretary General of the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), marking a significant shift in...

item.title: SpaceX's Starmind Data Centers and Starlink Satellite Expansion
item.description: SpaceX has shifted its focus from Mars to lunar and orbital data centers, launching the Starmind project with plans for 100,000 Starlink satellites. The initiative, now rebranded as Starmind AI1, feat...

item.title: Capitol Hill Women Use Whisper Networks to Navigate Harassment Risks from Lawmakers
item.description: A new report from The Bulwark highlights how young women on Capitol Hill navigate Washington's social scene, where off-hours drinks are a fixture of the power ecosystem. However, these interactions ca...
